text.title: Client Firing Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Client Firing Protocol application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Use a written checklist: (a) chronic scope abuse, (b) payment >30 days late ≥3 times, (c) abusive comms to team, (d) mission-mismatch with niche, (e) margin <baseline ≥6 months. ≥2 criteria = fire.
rule.rationale: Without checklist, firing decisions get rationalized in either direction; ≥2-criteria gate prevents emotional + complacent decisions.

rule.statement: Send termination in writing with ≥30-day notice (or contractual minimum, whichever is longer). Professional, not personal; reference contract clause.
rule.rationale: Verbal firings get re-litigated; written notice is the legal anchor + closes the loop cleanly.

rule.statement: Deliver a handover package (artefacts, credentials in secure vault, runbook, recommended successors) BEFORE last invoice. Make the next agency's life easy.
rule.rationale: Burning bridges is short-sighted; clean handover preserves reputation + creates referral source (yes, even from fired clients).

rule.statement: Final invoice + outstanding balance paid in full BEFORE handover delivery. No exceptions for 'we'll pay after we see the handover'.
rule.rationale: Last leverage moment; once handover is delivered, collection rate drops to ~50%.

rule.statement: Within 14 days, write a post-mortem: what signs were missed at intake, what cost was paid, what intake filter would have caught this. Pattern-recognize across past firings.
rule.rationale: One bad client is bad luck; two with similar profile is a pattern in your intake.
